claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for UK Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Let's begin the eligibility assessment. I'll need to ask you some questions about your situation.

**First, let's talk about your child:**

How old is the child you're claiming Child Benefit for?

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

If you're claiming for multiple children, we can go through each one. Let's start with the first child - what is their age?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, hi! Thanks for helping me with this. I'm asking about my son Alex - he's 13 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great to meet you! Thanks for that information about Alex. Since Alex is 13 years old, he falls into the **under 16** category, which is good news for eligibility purposes.

Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about Alex:**

Is Alex working **less than 24 hours per week** in paid employment (or not working at all)?

This is usually straightforward for a 13-year-old, as most children this age aren't in significant paid work. Just let me know if Alex has any regular paid employment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't work at all - he's still in school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that's what I expected for a 13-year-old still in school. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is Alex **not** receiving certain benefits in his own name? Specifically, is he free from receiving:
- Employment and Support Allowance (ESA), or
- Universal Credit

in his own right?

(For most 13-year-olds, the answer would be yes - they typically don't receive these benefits themselves.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, he doesn't get any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, that's another box ticked. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is Alex currently in an apprenticeship?

Since you mentioned he's still in school at 13, I expect the answer is no - but I just need to confirm he's **not** enrolled in any kind of apprenticeship programme.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not doing an apprenticeship - just regular school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, Alex is in regular school with no apprenticeship. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Now let's check your residency status:**

To be eligible for Child Benefit, you need to meet UK residency requirements. You can qualify if **any** of the following apply to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. You have **settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have **pre-settled status** and are working/earning above the National Insurance threshold
4. You have **pre-settled status** and are actively seeking work
5. You have **pre-settled status** with sufficient resources to support yourself
6. You have **pre-settled status** and are a student with sufficient resources
7. You have **pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. You're a **Crown servant** living abroad

The most common situation is simply that you normally live in the UK. Do any of these apply to you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Actually, I don't live in the UK - I'm abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thanks for letting me know. That's okay - there are still some circumstances where you might qualify.

**Can I ask a bit more about your situation?**

Are you living abroad as a **Crown servant** (for example, working for the UK government, armed forces, or diplomatic service overseas)?

Or are you living in certain qualifying countries due to your work?

If not, do you have any connection to the UK through the **EU Settlement Scheme** (either settled or pre-settled status)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant or anything like that. I don't have any EU Settlement Scheme status either - I'm just living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. Unfortunately, based on what you've told me, this means you don't meet the UK residency requirements for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I've now gathered enough information to give you an outcome.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## Child Benefit Eligibility Result

### ❌ Not Eligible

I'm sorry, but based on the information you've provided, **you are not currently eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

### Summary

| Child | Age | Eligible? | Reason |
|-------|-----|-----------|--------|
| Alex | 13 | ❌ No | Claimant does not meet residency requirements |

### Explanation

While **Alex** meets all the requirements to be a qualifying child (he's under 16, in school, not working, and not receiving benefits in his own name), the issue is with **your residency status**.

To claim Child Benefit, **you** must:
- Normally live in the UK, **or**
- Have settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with certain conditions), **or**
- Be a Crown servant living abroad, **or**
- Be living in certain qualifying countries

Since you're living abroad without any of these qualifying circumstances, you unfortunately cannot claim Child Benefit at this time.

### What You Can Do

- **If your circumstances change** (e.g., you return to live in the UK), you may become eligible and can apply at that point
- If another person in the UK is responsible for Alex's care, **they** may be able to claim Child Benefit for him instead
- For more information, you can visit: [GOV.UK Child Benefit Eligibility](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility)

I'm sorry I couldn't bring better news. Is there anything else you'd like me to clarify?

reasonings: Alex (age 13) meets all the child-related eligibility criteria: he is under 16, not working, not receiving benefits in his own name, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant (parent) does not meet the UK residency requirements. They are living abroad and are not a Crown servant, do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and do not fall into any other qualifying category for overseas claimants. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible to receive Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is asking about Child Benefit for one child, Alex (age 13). While Alex qualifies as an eligible child, the claimant does not meet the residency requirements as they are living abroad without any qualifying status (Crown servant, EU Settlement Scheme, etc.). UK residency or a qualifying exception is a mandatory requirement for receiving Child Benefit.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.

However, the claimant (parent) does not meet the UK residency requirements. They are living abroad and are not a Crown servant, do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and do not fall into any other qualifying category for overseas claimants. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible to receive Child Benefit for Alex.

While Alex qualifies as an eligible child, the claimant does not meet the residency requirements as they are living abroad without any qualifying status (Crown servant, EU Settlement Scheme, etc.).
